trending.title: EU merger shake-up exposes von der Leyen–Ribera clash of visions
trending.body: Commission chief promotes expansive “European champions” agenda as her Spanish No. 2 defends the foundational rules of the EU single market.

trending.title: EU countries cool on Brussels age-check app
trending.body: The EU’s app to check the age of internet users faces resistance from national governments, according to a survey by POLITICO.

trending.title: White House presses tech companies for support on AI-driven cyberattacks
trending.body: Tech and cyber companies were sent questions about artificial intelligence-led cybersecurity threats, including those posed by Anthropic’s advanced AI model, Mythos.

trending.title: White House’s ‘lack of organization’ has AI lobbyists fretting
trending.body: The Trump administration has sent mixed signals about how tough it intends to be in vetting powerful new computer models.

trending.title: Belgium urges EU to save industry by getting tough on China
trending.body: On returning from China, Foreign Minister Maxime Prévot warns the EU will become a “coalition of frogs” unless it embraces a cohesive strategy.

competition.title: EU merger shake-up exposes von der Leyen–Ribera clash of visions
competition.body: Commission chief promotes expansive “European champions” agenda as her Spanish No. 2 defends the foundational rules of the EU single market.

privacy.title: EU countries cool on Brussels age-check app
privacy.body: The EU’s app to check the age of internet users faces resistance from national governments, according to a survey by POLITICO.

regulatory.title: White House’s ‘lack of organization’ has AI lobbyists fretting
regulatory.body: The Trump administration has sent mixed signals about how tough it intends to be in vetting powerful new computer models.

regulatory.title: Belgium urges EU to save industry by getting tough on China
regulatory.body: On returning from China, Foreign Minister Maxime Prévot warns the EU will become a “coalition of frogs” unless it embraces a cohesive strategy.

regulatory.title: Germany set to secure win for industry in AI talks
regulatory.body: Exemption for industry would mark a dramatic departure from law’s initial setup.

regulatory.title: US firms could face exclusion under new EU cyber bill, lead lawmaker says
regulatory.body: “I can see the Cybersecurity Act having an impact on U.S. companies if they don’t oblige by the rules,” says Czech Pirate MEP Markéta Gregorová.

regulatory.title: Cloud law will stop Europe becoming tech ‘colony,’ Commission official says
regulatory.body: The fresh comments suggest Brussels plans to take a tough approach to wean the continent off foreign technology.

regulatory.title: European industry fears ‘back door’ for US in cloud law
regulatory.body: A technical formula on what counts as sovereign is prompting fierce reaction.

regulatory.title: EU pressure builds on Anthropic over Mythos hacking risks
regulatory.body: Officials are losing patience over lack of access to superhacking model.

regulatory.title: Key EU clash looms over Trump trade deal
regulatory.body: The U.S. president’s threat to hike tariffs on European autos supports the judgment of a majority of EU lawmakers that he can’t be trusted.

regulatory.title: PornHub lets UK Apple users back on its platform
regulatory.body: PornHub partially blocked access for U.K. users in January over concerns about the Online Safety Act.

regulatory.title: Europe’s laws ‘ill-equipped’ to deal with superhacking AI, lawmakers warn
regulatory.body: EU cyber agency ENISA needs access to Mythos to scrutinize the risks, lawmakers said.

regulatory.title: UK seeks access to EU’s €5B tech scale-up fund
regulatory.body: Ursula von der Leyen and Keir Starmer announce that negotiations are set to commence.

regulatory.title: A runaway tycoon and millions in crypto: Polish scandal stokes Tusk-Nawrocki fight
regulatory.body: Escalating battle over regulation fuels acrimony between Poland’s center-right prime minister and its nationalist president.

regulatory.title: EU accused of wasting €20B on AI computing dreams
regulatory.body: Critics argue that there’s no demand for the massive computing hubs Brussels has planned.

regulatory.title: France and Spain want space reserved for EU firms in satellite frequencies
regulatory.body: Brussels and EU capitals are mulling restrictions on non-EU players.

regulatory.title: China threatens to retaliate over EU self-reliance push
regulatory.body: Beijing says it stands ready to act if Brussels muscles it out of the bloc’s single market.

regulatory.title: EU must have more of its own taxes from 2028, says von der Leyen
regulatory.body: Commission president calls for next EU budget to have more money that doesn’t come from national coffers.

regulatory.title: Brussels tells EU countries to use its age-check app
regulatory.body: Security experts found holes in the EU’s tech earlier, and capitals are reluctant to adopt Brussels’ tools.

regulatory.title: EU says Meta needs to do more to keep under-13s off Instagram and Facebook
regulatory.body: Brussels is under pressure to protect kids online as countries move forward with social media bans.

regulatory.title: EU legislators fail to clinch deal to delay AI law
regulatory.body: Talks derailed over request backed by Germany to weaken rules for manufacturing and medical devices.

regulatory.title: UK calls on ‘middle powers’ to counter over-concentration of AI control
regulatory.body: The technology secretary said Britain is making a “decisive move” towards backing its own startups.
